title: angelica callii
intro: Angelica callii, also known as Mexican masterwort, is a perennial plant that belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae) and is native to Mexico and the southwestern United States. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic effects. The plant's roots, leaves, and stems are used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections, offering benefits such as reduced inflammation, relief from menstrual cramps, and support for immune function. Angelica callii can be prepared as herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, making it a versatile remedy for various health concerns.
